channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: spaceX IPO — 257 replies — 1028.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 257 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:34 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358139
>>62357966
>The racks are powered by PMDC switch gear with backup diesel generators. You need backup power and the ability to seamless transition to gen power so you dont have outages. This space or ocean data centers are complete nonsense.
my guy....

have you not asked yourself why they use diesel generators or need backup power in the first place....

show me the finished ai hyperscalers. how many MW or GW do we have

62358152
>>62358056
solar underwater? lol good luck

62358158
it's up

I REPEAT

THE SCAM IS UP


I WAS HERE

i was also at my PC when the GTA 6 trailer leaked and when TTWO decided to drop theirs. it was 45 minutes later iirc

62358182
>>62358137
you'll be singing a very different tune when spacex's floating garbage dump makes low earth orbit too dangerous to traverse

62358193
just +22% wtf it should be +100% at least

62358202
robinhood is down lmao

62358206
the motherfucker did it again. praise godking elon.

62358213
The goy milking has started

62358225
>>62351469
I think they will lure in as much exit liquidity as possible before the rug

62358259
>>62351581
Elon is on team Jew. You have fallen for the Elon Musk [ based and redpilled propaganda, being susceptible to propaganda is a sign of low IQ

62358280
>>62358139
>have you not asked yourself why they use diesel generators or need backup power in the first place
No, because I know why. Equipment failure at the data center. A PMDC transformer can fail to close on power up failing to deliver medium voltage to the switch gear to go out to the server floor and power the racks. A diesel gen will kick on to supply power until the control room operators switch to a different PMDC on line power to power those racks. Once off the PMDC with the issue it can be troubleshot and corrected. There are hundreds of PMDCs and gens pairs in some of these data centers like Googles SLN site.

62358313
Oppenheimer Buy Initiates $190 +19.50% Jun 11, 2026

62358326
retail squares getting dumped on

62358331
>>62358206
dude. why is no one ever mentioning how badly that little autistic Trump boy wants to laugh.
look how hard he's suppressing his laugh with his larynx/trachea. watch the mouth too

apparently his slavic bitch mother told him "you need to be stoic in public. smiling shows weakness" ala Kanye
god the slavs are fucking cucks and delusional

62358388
>>62358225
It's gonna be up for 15 days at least, until it hits the index. That's when the ride starts

62358408
I might have bought in if it was cheaper but seriously, 2 trillion market cap right from the get go?
No way that's gonna go up any time soon, it'll likely plummet all the way down instead.

62358446
>>62358259
You are on team China. You have fallen for the Chinese [ based and redpilled propaganda, being susceptible to antisemitism is a sign of low IQ

62358479
>>62358027
you cant cool shit in space, the only way is by radiation
and that very slow and need bg ass radiators

just look at the ISS it make all thoses white 10 panels it has are radiators coolen about 70kw of head

for a data center in space you would need thausens on km2 of radiators to cool it down

by the way the ISS is t humankinds most expencive singel object ever build
just building a datacenter in space would need 1000-2000 times more money then the ISS did cost

and maintans good luck launching bob 1-3 time a week into space to fix the servers or replace broken parts..

Underwater data centers located near the coast are more realistic, they can generate energy through movement of the see, but maintenance would pose a major problem if they were too far offshore

cost to build it asside...

62358502
>>62357383
ok, and how much shit do you have to get up into orbit and at what cost just to replace a single "data center" on Earth? We have THOUSANDS of data centers already and more being built every day, the proposal is to send shit up to orbit at an incredibly high cost, and not be able to repair / replace tens of thousands of satellites easily and the tech in them will get outdated fast as you can see what's happening now every time there's a new leap in chip manufacturing and these data centers are all chasing the newest tech upgrades to stay ahead.

It's completely nonsensical, the entire argument is muh free energy from the sun, OK fine, how are you going to send all the mass up there just to collect all that energy and power enough infrastructure to make it work when you can't launch that much shit to orbit and starship hasn't even made orbit yet or delivered a single payload? The entire thing is a scam for hype

62358521
I think this space narrative retardation has legs. You know the AIfags that think the ability to ask a chatbot an infantile question and it fetching the top Google result is amazing? The same ones that are like
>AI robots are going to take all the jobs bro
>AI + robotics
>automation you just wait
When we already have robotics and automation. As if hooking a chatbot up to a welding robot making cuckovers in Michigan and giving it the knowledge base of reddit is going to do anything useful
>b-but HUMANOID robots + AI
Lol, lmao even. These same retards are screeching
>data centers BUT IN SPACE connected with LASERS and and they're CLUSTERS
It's the dumbest shit imaginable but look at the retail mania in chatbots 2.0 that are nothing more than a mildly interesting tool yet they think we have invented an artificial super intelligence. Dumb space stonks. This is it. This is the next big retarded thing to try and extend the top.

62358524
>>62358479
lol i already debunked see >>62357919
>muh iss
so predictable
u r dumb

62358529
>>62358479
My favorite part about that is that he wants to launch 1 fucking million of them. I did the math for fun and with conservative estimates, that's like 100k sats above the visible horizon at any given time. More than one satellite per square degree of the night sky.
Night would literally cease to be, not to mention the seething from from literally every other country that wants to launch something into space, ever

62358535
>>62358502
>at an incredibly high cost
lol no
starship nigger
less than $200 a kg to leo

62358541
>>62358535
That number is Elon's delusional wish, not reality moron.

62358564
>>62358535
Fuck off Elon you will never be an engineer, stick to your 3D renders

62358569
>>62356541
why does it matter for them, image how much it would have to crash for them to lose money.

62358581
>>62358569
Doesn't matter for them, it matters for the retail baggies

62358585
>22.2%
Another day Mr Musk has made me wealth

62358623
>>62358541
well i speculate it will be a reality
starship will work, it will be fully reusable and mass produced. and you will find a new cope

>>62358564
nobody was mad about spacex until elon backed trump and said you will never be a real woman. since then endless daily seethe about starship. its hard to take seriously

over 500 successful landings on falcon 9 boosters btw. and you think spacex doesn't know what they're doing lol. you have elon derangement syndrome.

take a deep breathe, dilate, and respond without sounding mad.

62358633
>>62358023
Might as well ride and exit before it pops

item.title: SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:31 UTC

OP

Kek fudniggers

Early replies (first 3)

62354945
>Inb4 source
https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971
it's all so tiring
i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943
Cool, when will I be rich?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62356154
>>62356084
>specifically MESSAGE orchestration
kek this is how you know it's a xerpie writing this.
They love pointing out how Chainlink is "only for messages" while XRP is for settlement.

>>62355613
What the fuck is that even supposed to mean?

62356178
>>62355053
>just 3 more years marines! HODL!

62356202
>>62356144
Your post qualified it as a *finally* given confirmation which was itself announced almost a year ago. I am not even arguing for a single coin here, I am just saying the consortium trust model is totally irrelevant now and inferior to automated trust that settles faster and cheaper with substantially less risk. Definitely super organic that within 3 minutes of making my post in this thread in the early morning, I am spam replied to by 3 different IDs none of which even reply to my point. How much is CLL paying brown hands to type nowadays?
>>62356149
Wtf are you talking about? What about what I said was even vaguely conceptually dense or difficult such that you think it would take AI to write or conceive of it? Do you realize how stupid this makes you sound?
>>62356154
I didn't even mention XRP but how does incredulously restating the point I made argue against its validity?

62356205
>>62354943

Dreadfully distinct against the dark

interlinked

A tall white fountain played

62356226
>>62356202
>I didn't even mention XRP
I just explained to you how you outed yourself.

>how does incredulously restating the point I made argue against its validity?
99% of modern banking is "message orchestration". Trying to pretend money isn't made here is just hilarious.

62356241
>>62354976
unironically 2035

62356274
>>62356202
You are the insufferable verbose XRP loser that shits out entire paragraphs of nonsense that noone (SWIFT most of all) cares about except other baggies like yourself. Tell us, is the boom coming up soon? Everytime you say "settlement", "nostro vostro" or "deep liquidity" we have to take a shot.

62356321
>>62356226
>Outted myself
Wut?? This is an insane level of melodrama and headcanonizing instead of just admitting you don't understand the doc's diagram or what it means for the token itself. I never said message orchestration was not a profit center, I said it was not a profit center for token holders. Chainlink Labs will make a clean margin to the extent they are used here in what volume exists to be used for as a business entity. That doesn't mean anything to or for you unless you are a shareholder.
>>62356274
Genuinely don't know what you are talking about or how you think this replies to what I said? I haven't said any of these terms and all you have done is get really mad and try to completely evade the point of the thread. Anything which doesn't just auto-agree despite whether the engine matches the hood advertised is an assault on your identity and proof against the engine's congruity is like unto saying ywnbaw to trannies and must be scorned and repressed as quickly and through as much obvious weird emotional fury as possible!

62356407
>>62356321
You are that XRP guy who writes a lot?

Good luck with ripple dude, I'm sure you will be fine.

62356412
don't you get it stinky
it doesn't matter
value does not accrue to the token
it's all for fat sergey and his harem of fat HR roasties (his personal fetish)
this is how private companies like chainlink labs work. you hold NO legal ability to claim ANY share of these profits because muh chainlink token means NOTHING in the eyes of the law

62356517
>>62356412
>you hold NO legal ability to claim ANY share of these profits
Because that's why people buy crypto right, the profit sharing?
top fucking kek

62356547
>>62356517
value
does
not
accrue
to
token

chainlink could power the whole damn world and the token will still be $7

62356570
>>62356547
that's true for all crypto without mass adoption

62356591
>>62356570
Wrong.
What you just did is called cope.

62356612
>>62356591
not one crypto has ever pumped on systemic demand.
It's all been exclusively speculative

62356755
>>62356612
sooo whats ur point, stinkie? that link sucks ASS? yes i agree

62356985
>>62356755
idk what the fuck my point is? whats ur point?

62357019
>>62356985
my point is simple:
fuck stinks

62357032
>>62357019
based (i misread the og msg my nig)

62357138
>>62354943
interlinked

62357233
>>62354943
space x ipo is a cover for link to moon. cap this

62357678
>>62356407
You are very strange and cognitively dissonant.

62357689
$8 soon LFG marines

62357734
>>62356178
He asked

62357861
>>62357734
and you answered
in a cringey pathetic whiney desperate way
but you answered

62357965
As a Link holder please get close with Jesus Christ. Because shits about to get insane.

62358109
>>62354943
token...NEEDED?

62358124
>>62357965
yes, Lord Jesus can help guide you through your life of pain, suffering, and poverty

62358239
>>62356084
>iTs oNly fOr mEsSaGiNg

Under cover xerper.

62358599
>right about absolutely EVERYTHING except price
lol, lmao even

item.title: spacex = $2 Trillion dollars — 23 replies — 86.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
spacex = $2 Trillion dollars

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:18 UTC

OP

MARKET CAP WORTH $2 TRILLION DOLLARS RIGHT NOW @ $164

LMFAO

Early replies (first 3)

62358200
>>62358179
boomers are right, paying attention to this fake and gay stock market is retarded

just invest in an index fund every month and forget

62358208
>>62358200
honestly they were fucking right the entire time, I should've listened to my father-in-law

62358232
>>62358179
it'll be the biggest crash in history. And that's not a prediction, it's a spoiler.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358264
>>62358200
>just invest in an index fund every month and forget
they are buying SPCX too LAMO

62358266
>>62358179
Wait and see it drops to less than $1 trillion in a week.

62358268
soon it'll be double digits

62358282
it's dumping to fuck LMAO

62358291
>>62358179
ALWAYS inverse reddit. Always.

62358339
Reminds me of the South Sea stock bubble.

>muh SPACE!!
>muh EXPLORATION!!

it's a completely hollow and speculative corporation

62358364
>>62358291
what is reddit saying?

62358370
>ITT seething gay bears who don't get to make an easy 20%

62358387
the entire economy is fake and gay.

62358395
>>62358179
>MARKET CAP WORTH $2 TRILLION DOLLARS RIGHT NOW @ $164
Except that is only a small portion of the shares. If 100% of shares were publicly traded the price pressure would collapse so it really doesn't have $2 trillion market cap. It's a part of the scam of IPOs

62358404
I believe Space X is the corporation that will bring on the planned global crash and economic reset.

That's the only purpose of Space X

62358424
Also I believe Trump rushed the news of an "imminent signing" with Iran just to prop up this Space X IPO.

The timing is just amusing. The supposed deal with Iran is already falling apart now that the IPO happened

62358427
>>62358200
that's a solid approach if you have a decent job and only plan to retire around maybe your mid fifties earliest, probably a bit later.
it requires basically zero brainpower to pull off too, you can just automate it.
but it will never make you significantly richer than your regular income already made you anyway, which is what most of /biz/ is trying to aim for.

62358444
>>62358339
I just never re-enter, at least not without some serious time delay or actual changes that justify the change in price.
If I pulled out then I did so for a reason, and I'll just look for something new.

62358451
I pity all of you who don't get on the Tulip Wagon in time.

62358454
>>62358404
>planned global crash
how does a 550 million float on a company bring planetary economic crash?

62358455
>>62358444
checked

62358465
>>62358454
fuck you just trust me on this.

Damn, you guys just can't let a doomer doompost a lil bit

62358467
>>62358454
Well here’s how Burry explains it, though of course he’s been constantly predicting crashes ever since back when he was right once
https://x.com/BullTheoryio/status/2061029970236133554

62358473
>>62358395
Smells like any vc scam crypto tokenomics from israel.

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:32 UTC

OP

COINBASE NOW ALLOWS AI AGENTS TO TRADE CRYPTO AUTONOMOUSLY

Early replies (first 3)

62352704
Institutions were doing this 4+ years ago, will change nothing.

62352713
Who care Robin hood did it first

Also I think thos agentic stuff might be overhyped, I ran bots before and idk why I would switch to an ai that hallucinates

62352715
>>62352687
Sharper /pol/ + /b/‑style mock

“NUCLEAR HAPPENING”
Calm down, doomsday prophet — Coinbase letting AI trade crypto isn’t the Cuban Missile Crisis.

You’re acting like ChatGPT just seized the launch codes when all it did was place a limit order on DOGE at 3 AM.

This isn’t Skynet, it’s a robo‑intern buying your bags because you can’t stop doomscrolling long enough to click “confirm trade.”

The only meltdown here is your brain trying to process a press release.

Even sharper (still safe)

Bro saw “AI agents can rebalance your portfolio” and immediately started building a fallout shelter out of Amazon boxes.

Coinbase: adds a convenience feature

OP: “THE MACHINES HAVE RISEN.”

Relax, Nostradamus. The AI isn’t overthrowing humanity — it’s just making sure you stop panic‑selling at the bottom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352728
i will outcompete them easily

market buy @40K
market sell @180K

I'm the most handsome man on 4chan

62352738
>>62352687
AI can't do that yet, so "agent" is the correct word, i.e. a model that can't trade gets stupid bandaid skills context from users, in the hope to bait them into losing money.
First thing I did with the new v5 model from Anthropic is stock assessment, it's still too weakly willed to form an opinion, it still just mirrors your sentiment.

62352773
>>62352687
nobody cares about "muh crypto" any more boomer. wake up

62352788
>>62352773

8
shadow
not first edition

into the trash it goes

62352838
>>62352788
k ill take it out of your trash

62352840
>>62352788
Should I get it graded?

62352936
>>62352773
>nobody cares
Pretty much this. Not even coinbase has that kind of pull anymore to get people’s attention. Base was OK in the beginning but it turned out to be yet another liquidity farm for insiders. For years, crypto had a bad rep but the last couple of years, along with the sol shitcoin casino, basically finished it off.

62352979
>>62352773
>now the cryptards will try to kill pokemon for threatening their ecosystem

62353423
>>62352773
You really need that faggot chain to work out huh. You've been shilling for the last week. KYS people on this board trade metals,crypto, and stock. That is a mentally retarded tranny "asset" I will NEVER view artificial scarcity trading cards as an investment.

62353565
>>62352687
this was happening their first year of the Coinbase foundation in 2015, moron.

62355014
>>62352773
Wait I have one of these, how much are these worth? What's the bar code thing, I assume the 8 is a score?

62355083
>>62352715
>—

62355640
>>62352687
as opposed of what? the vast amount of trading (in all assets) are done by AI algos.

62358618
>>62352773
>>62352840
shit you guys I didn't realize these were worth money, I have a complete shadowless set gemini says it's probably worth around 6k even though the cards are a bit worn. thanks for reminding me of this.

item.title: ITS ALREADY CRASHING — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITS ALREADY CRASHING

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:32 UTC

OP

ITS ALREADY CRASHING

62358286
>>62358276
So this is it, it's the bubble burst. I'd never imagine I'd see this live

62358302
>>62358286
nope, we still have openAI and anthropic to IPO. we need them to inflate this harder so it's in our best interest that spacex just keep pumping into full psychosis until they IPO then "reality" can hit and we can reset to the stone ages.

62358306
>>62358276
4 trillion EOD

62358320
It's up 20% from the IPO price, lmao

62358347
>>62358276
imagine if it just bleeds to $100 today

62358360
>>62358276
Sold and got an easy 22% profit, will buy in again when it crashes down to $60
See ya baggies

62358374
>>62358302
>>62358347
if it drops below 150, it's over retail is screwed, confidence in big IPOs is shattered and we can for sure assure the bubble burst.

62358375
>>62358276
Do niggers not understand basic money supply theory? If you think this shit will moon and do a 10x then you are a certified retarded

62358378
Doom sissies know nothing. $400 minimum.

62358411
>>62358320

62358420
we're back

62358587
>>62358360

62358609

humble brag

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:31 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62356197
>>62356048
So who owns it really? Someone with old money?

62356653
>>62356048
(You) don’t own that any more than I do big guy

62356703
>>62356653
he's european so he enjoys being a slave if it means he can be proud about some guy's tacky villa in the mediterranean

62357456
>>62356048
Give me 100 drones and I could destroy that property in like half an hour. Or rent a plane a smear liquid shit all over that mansion lol
>he does whatever he wants! He has...3 passports! properties around the...le world!
Niggers' idea of wealth is truly funny. Knowledge and wisdom are the true wealth in this life.

62357608
>>62356048
Sure but that means you’re Jewish and bound for the pit

62358258
>>62356048
You have a point. American billionaires have the worst taste imaginable

62358413
>go to bed
>wake up to orders in my online store
>hundreds of dollars in profit while I slept
This is the most bare bones no effort business I could stumble in to. It's not impossible

62358433
>>62357456
>wisdom
then why are you here poorfag? theres no wisdom in this shithole.

62358436
>>62356048
Sneed

62358457
>>62356197
>So who owns it really?
The Académie des Beaux-Arts. You can visit the villa if you want. It's like a public museum at this point.

62358606
>>62356048
>AAAACKKKKKKK

item.title: XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-12 16:32 UTC

OP

Some breaking xrp news

Early replies (first 3)

62351536
Remember when Brad said he didnt want to support the clarity act?

62351601
>>62351406
Why do you keep making these corny linkie threads to cope with objectively losing?
>>62351536
He literally never said that. He said he would support a bill which served crypto and would oppose one which didn't in the very early days of the formulation when the Clarity Act was leaning a bad direction. He has literally posted to mark up and pass it repeatedly and been lobbying for its passage on mainstream news on Fox Business. Why would you choose to be so low IQ and frustrated rather than change your thesis based on what's true? INSANE cope.

62354640
>>62351406
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355362
>>62351406
So bullish

62355367
>>62351406
Did we get our assets handed to us?

62355473
>>62351601
Why do you keep posting this chart as some sort of gotcha when it shows you're down 70% from ATH?

62356152
>>62355473
Because it demonstrates my point completely and I know it makes you mad and that you are bad at coping with the madness it causes. The entire broad market is crushed and still XRP in the most recent change of guard is crushing everything else and over 100% up. Why do you stay in positions you know have no real thesis behind them to cope instead of changing based on what's true? My dream is that the entire market crashes 90% more to buy one last time providing nothing essential caused it outside valuation washouts as has been the case because the crypto I invest in has an actual purpose and the crypto you invest in has only marketing and is literally designed to get you to buy to fund corporate ops.

62356157
>>62351406
Are those the chainlink colours? Very bullish

62356164
>>62356152
This post triggered me so I’m giving you a (you). Just know you’re one of the dumbest niggers around if you actually believe what you posted.

62358620
>>62356164
You are as a community down 70% on average on the position you hold and 90% from ATH 6 years ago despite only launching a few years before that. In the new crypto paradigm post-Chokepoint with ETH no longer being carried by lawfare where it cannot go on tech, LINK is down more against HBAR and XLM and XRP than the most legendary stock traders in history made in their careers investing and link is down in proper terms vs. the dollar. Cope.

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 39 replies — 24.3/hr

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-12 14:58 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349956
the next ubisoft

62349957
>>62348037
you could have made 100k scalping pokemon cards, wtf are you doing OP

62349964
>>62348037
Japan as a society is super conservative and stuck in its way to a retarded degree, what do you expect from the land of fax machines and archipelago syndrome?

62350244
>>62349911
What stocks options are you buying bro?

62350352
>>62348037
>all good games are made by indie developers
why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies

62350621
Thanks for piquing my interest. Now I am off to do some research. What exactly is it that they are doing to piss you off? Other than losing sole rights to Pokémon cards?

62350665
>>62348037
I tried to tell you.

No, literally, I tried to. I was the one /v/ fag screaming at you that nintendo was on its way down the same path that killed ubisoft and ruined video games.

62350696
>>62348037
slurp the dip

62350697
>>62348037
>actually investing real money in Bing Bing wahoo

62350703
>>62350665
I am not too familiar with the video game industry. I considered to buy Ubisoft then decided not to a while ago. Had other things that ended up going for instead. Maybe I should wait out the tech bubble popping. But I do love pre-dumped stocks.

62350739
Zooming out this just looks like it is at the 2022-3 lows. The bull run happened last year. Investing in an economy in another country. Their bull runs will often have a different timing. However this can clearly go down a lot more.

62350816
>>62348037
Did you use the Switch 2 for a bunch of hours? Yes? So you saw a device with a 2-hour battery life and no games, but you still decided to go all-in on this company?

62350869
Nintendo used to put out sub par hardware that still managed to be hugely successful in the console wars, they had the handheld market cornered, 3ds was kino. How the decision to kill and eat Gameboy with a retarded tablet console got through will always be perplexing to me.

62350907
>>62350816
Ouch. Released in 2025. That caused it to dump. No wonder that bull run was so short. Nintendo bull runs usually last over a year and don't go down that fast. I think this company is recoverable but I do not know what they plan on doing to make better products. Will this be like HP and Ubisoft being shit and making bad decisions until they get a new CEO and clean house? Scratch that. Forgot what was going on at Ubisoft that made me 2nd guess buying.

62351015
Oh now I know why I didn't invest in Ubisoft is because the different ticker types were too confusing. That on top of shady incompetence going on right now.

62351629
stop buying with emotions and hopes
rape these companies like they would rape you

gaming companies at release you always short. even TTWO

62351972
>>62348037
Why are chinks like this?

62352012
>>62350907
Switch 2 is probably just a transition console before their next gimmick, like the 3DS or Wii U. Still, it could have been done much better.

62352040
>>62348263
Travis Scott had a concert in Fortnite.

62352596
>>62352012
This is always part of the problem with investing in companies normies have heard of and are emotional. I am beginning to preferer investing in reindustrialization. But videogame companies sure are damn fun to invest in from the looks of it. I was getting bored to death looking up the next best machinery parts company to invest in. Too poor to invest in all of them that aren't complete shit or too high and hope for the best. Have to really narrow it down to a few at some point. Very tedious. Especially for an average retail fag. But Nintendo and Ubisoft I can afford multiple shares.

62352642
>>62349809
Lol. Not if you just keep holding, idiot. But that's what you get for investing in bing bing wahoo slop.

62352731
>>62350352
I don't know Apple had a big ass rebound thanks to Steve Jobs. Long history old companies having a comeback after years if not decades of mismanagement.

62354514
>>62350703
This is the conundrum of just recently getting enough money to theoretically invest in something because you're a zoomer. Tech has been pumped to hell and back already and the risk of buying in now and winding up near the top is worse than just letting inflation eat into savings.
It might continue going for years to come I'm aware but there's no way this is sustainable without a crash at some point.

62354552
>>62348177
Switch 2 is the best selling console and selling at faster pace than Switch 1, the upcoming games like new Ocarina of Time, CoD, Pokemon wind/waves are going to push the console hard

62354555
>>62354514
Millennial. I haven't been keeping up with what is going on the video game industry lately. Because I hardly play anymore. The thing is researching machine parts companies is tedious. Nintendo and Ubisoft are practically rock bottom low and easier to understand what they do. Already made money off of mining stocks. Just a smart poor fag being a poor fag carefully picking out what to invest in. Because I can't invest in everything I want to on my wishlist.

62354579
>>62352012
They ran out of feasible gimmicks that's why it's been the same shit repackaged since wiiU

62354587
Hahaha, are you the fucking retard that shilled them before the direct?
Classic!

62354609
I'm buying at this level, thanks for helping me fill my position bro

62357401
>>62354552
people have lost their creativity. nothing new like these classics come out, only slop using the brand of something people recognize but no one out there creating new zeldas and pokemons

62357443
>>62348037
Nintendo is sitting on an assload of cash. they don't care about shareholders.

item.title: BTC — 16 replies — 11.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-12 15:12 UTC

OP

Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62347134
>>62347031
so they can continuously pump and dump and pretend it's simply "natural" and "mathematical" and whatever other bullshit marketing they do

62347139
>>62347031
>cycles
Human psychology

>exchange
Kraken

>wallet
Your memory, but I’ll let other people shill their products below

62347162
>>62347139
He's new to it. Start with the Bluewallet app on your phone, look for more secure alternatives as you go.

https://bitcoin.org/en/choose-your-wallet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347465
Thank you for your helpful responses frens

62347618
>>62347031
Its a self fulfilling prophecy at this point but it started because of the halving.

62347821
>>62347031
>Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
each 4 years there is a halving

>Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?
4chin censors the reply, probably site is run by some retards
b. core
change b. to full name of the coin
tripple check sources before install

source: og here

62347830
>>62347031

62348122
>>62347821
So the "b . Org" is the wrong one?

I hate how predatory search engines are now that we are the product

62350049
>>62348122
bitcoin core is the real program, og

bitcoin core dot org is the site
but also/better check github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin

62350055
>>62348122
>>62350049
bitcoin.org is the original site, yes, it has a list of wallets, including the bitcoincore main wallet

to get rich/avoid getting poor:
- keep backups
- doublecheck backups
- make more backups
- document how you restore and use backups, so if you give up on btc and find this back in 10 years you can recover it still
- more backups, in case of a fire

- do NOT keep money on exchange, withdraw
- do NOT move all money at once. go slow. send, wait hours, verify, continue
- just buy weekly or "daily" (daily cost averaging). buy more if it falls a lot
- never sell it all

62350264
>>62350055
That is very helpful, thank you :)

62350298
>>62350055
checked && self custody is for retards.

62350304
>>62347139
>Human psychology
Jewish psychology

62350478
>>62350264
cheers

also avoid altcoins. they don't plug into a printer even

62354917
paper wallet

62357639
>>62347031
CEX is thrash, only go for BTCFI that keeps BTC native like babylon, yield is on the low side but it safer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62357023

62357041
>>62356906
I wasn’t expecting to get any. I’ve never participated in an IPO before. I’m sure it will sell off over the course of the next year or so but I plan on holding forever and hopefully add to my position.

62357048
>we gon' be rich cuh, space X is gon make me a millionAIRE cuh; finna buy a houze in beverly hillllzz and rent an escalade at 24.99%

62357064
kys if you bought:

chainlink
spaceX
ethereum
tesla

62357122
>>62357048
>>62357064
In an age when every company across America refuses to be innovative and actually address problems, instead opting for the status quo and having the government print money to give them, so I turn they can pursue social engineering projects instead, one guy has risen to the top. Elon Musk continue to dream. He continues to innovate. And despite the endless persecution from the media and leftist shills, he continues to succeed.
When Starship is ready to perform missions it will completely demolish competition. SpaceX is already out competing every other space company by delivering 2x+ cargo tonnage into space compared to its closest competitor.
Starship is far and away ahead of any rival company or even country’s most advanced space craft it’s actually absurd.

Yall can get mad all you want but you know I’m right. What company poses any serious challenge to SpaceX over the next decade? Blue Origin? They can’t even get people into actual space, or reliable land the re-usable portions of their rocket. United Launch alliance? Their largest rocket only lifts half the tonnage of the Falcon heavy. This isn’t a hard decision.

62357297
>>62357122
IPO price only makes sense if you look at 10+ years horizon. That's not how any other company in the world is valuated. If you're ok holding for 10 years, yeah sure it's an ok buy

62357323
>>62357122
>18 billion revenue (a loss)
>1.7 trillion valuation
lol.

62357656
>>62357023
Ooooohnononononono

62357700
>>62357122
Musk is getting more and more unhinged as he ages. It won't be long until his multiple lawsuits catch up with him.

62357725
>>62357323
They’re going to be space station builders, rocket ship manufacturers, and launch pad constructors. Every mansion in America won’t be complete without their launch pad, similar to what it is for helipads/private runways today.

62357741
>>62357122
copium

item.title: my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's,… — 22 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's,…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-12 14:34 UTC

OP

my mom bumped into a neighbor on a street we use to live in back in the 90s

her son was a literal chad, had 10/10 GF's, went to an elite private school, tall, handsome, etc

turns out he's now living in section 8 housing, addicted to online gambling and drugs

and all this time i imagined he had to have ended up as some super successful winner in life

Early replies (first 3)

62349865
you finally got your revenge anon. Ugly ass nerds victory, total chad death

62349886
>>62349860
"See? The Chad ended up in Section 8 housing and addicted to gambling and drugs. Therefore looks, popularity, and social success don't matter."
Hold your clown horses.
One guy crashing his life into a ditch doesn't magically prove that advantages aren't advantages. That's like seeing a former lottery winner go bankrupt and concluding money has no value. That's not an argument; that's a unicycle-powered statistical faceplant.
The guy started with a stack of advantages: attractiveness, social status, confidence, access to a good school, dating success, and likely a stronger social network than most people ever get. Those things are real advantages. They don't guarantee victory, but pretending they don't matter is pure cope flavored cotton candy.
What his story actually proves is that advantages are not invincibility shields.
A Ferrari can still be driven into a lake by a drunk driver.
The real clown move is taking an extreme outlier and treating it like a universal law.
"One Chad became a drug addict, therefore being attractive doesn't help."
Okay, and one billionaire lost everything in crypto. Does that mean wealth provides no benefits? One Olympic athlete became obese. Does that mean fitness is worthless?
Of course not.
The fact that someone squandered advantages doesn't erase the advantages.

If anything, the shocking part of the story is precisely because people expect someone with those advantages to do better than average. Nobody hears "handsome popular private-school kid became addicted to drugs and gambling" and thinks, "Yep, exactly the outcome I'd predict."
So no, the lesson isn't that success, looks, popularity, status, education, and opportunity are meaningless.
The lesson is that life is not deterministic.
Anyone using this story as proof that advantages don't matter is juggling statistics with oven mitts while wearing a clown nose and repeatedly riding their tricycle into the same wall.

62349924
>>62349860
Losing at life while playing on easy mode is really something else.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349929
>>62349886
lil chud really sat down and wrote a mini-essay that nobody is going to read

62349935
fellas, relax

im just saying in my head i imagined him being some super successful person now

we grew up playing together on the street, but eventually drifted a part as he became a chad and i became a chud

The funny thing is we probably have way more close of a relatable lifestyle now, sort of like how we did as kids

62349949
>>62349929
that's botslop

62349951
>>62349929
Chud used ChatGPT. Contains a couple of LLM syllogisms.

62349953
>>62349860
>>62349860
And WE are not addicted to online gambling? I lost 35% in Stocks and silver this year

62349965
>>62349953
no, WE are not

62349978
Which one Aron ? Idk anything beasidea parts of my story. Cheers lmao

62349987
Definitely didn’t read the mini book we just come here to laugh not read. Humor is the best medicine,

62350000
>>62349860
Why would a guys own mother say to her friend she hasn't seen in a long time that her son is a drug addict and a gambler? Ask yourself that, maybe this guy is so chad that he cut that bitch loose, all that parasitic weight on his shoulders, all the expectation, the nagging, the milestones which need to be hit for his mothers ego, the gaslighting and manipulation from his mother befriending his girlfriends and developing a seperate friendship with her where she can manipulate you through her turning your own wife into a version of your mother. Toxic family attatchments just dragging him the fuck down.

62350009
>>62349953
Thank you for your money.

62350016
>>62350000
Shitting on her son like that could be an indicator that she the real problem, but then it's equally true that women are retards in general and they just do random illogical shit. She could be some idiot NPC woman who loves her son but always rambles what's on her mind without ever thinking ahead. There's a lot of women like that.

62350140
>>62350000
My mom and his mom basically raised all of us together as neighbors, this was back in the 90's when neigh ours were actually close to each other, they basically had kids around the same time and all of us are the same age

We're also White, where the boomers talk to each other openly about their kids, not try to hide it like Asians or whatever

62350193
>>62349860
the halo effect is strong, but it's still a gamble, if the parents do zero nurturing, and they don't get picked up by a talent agent, then really they're just a wasted opportunity, which happens all the time in nature

62350225
Having a platform doesn't mean you can't fall off it, but it's better than having no platform.

62350233
>>62349886
being chad fries your dopamine early in life
chad constnat success life is like living on drugs, but then once the chad life drops even a little, he gets dopamine withdrawal and needs to use drugs

62350238
>>62349886
Yeah
>>62349860
Looks aren't everything. It can be a lot but it's not everything. Plenty of people peak in high school

62352332
>>62350140
Ok? So that is your reasoning as to why you are spiritually, socially and morally dominated by middle aged women.

62357174
>>62349860
It's how most of them end up.
People who peak early always rope, just be patient and in your late 20's early 30's anyone with some cash and basic talking skills can get tight pussy.
